A construction material calculator helps homeowners and contractors estimate how much concrete, paint, tile, gravel, roofing, drywall, asphalt, or bricks are needed for a project. MaterialCalcPro provides free, fast, and accurate construction material calculators to plan home renovation and small construction projects with confidence.

Construction Material Calculator for DIY Projects

MaterialCalcPro is a free construction material calculator designed for DIY builders, homeowners, and small contractors.

Use our calculators to quickly estimate material quantities for common home renovation and construction projects without complex formulas or spreadsheets.

Each calculator is built to provide fast, accurate, and easy-to-understand results, helping you avoid overbuying materials and reducing project waste.

Free Construction Calculators for Home Renovation

Our DIY construction calculators help estimate materials for:

  • Concrete slabs, footings, and bases
  • Paint coverage for walls and rooms
  • Floor tiles, roofing, asphalt, and gravel
  • Brick walls, fence posts, drywall, and flooring projects

All calculators support metric and imperial units and are optimized for real-world building scenarios.

How to Estimate Construction Materials Accurately

Estimating construction materials correctly depends on three key factors:

  • Surface area or volume
  • Material thickness or coverage rate
  • Waste allowance for cuts and uneven surfaces

MaterialCalcPro automatically applies standard formulas and optional waste factors to give reliable estimates for most DIY construction projects.

Why Use an Online Construction Material Calculator

Using an online construction calculator helps you:

  • Save time during project planning
  • Reduce material waste and cost overruns
  • Get quick estimates before purchasing supplies
  • Plan projects with confidence, even as a beginner

MaterialCalcPro is built to make construction planning simple, fast, and accessible.

pages.title: Concrete Calculator
pages.body_html:
Home

Instant concrete volume and bag estimate calculator for slabs and small projects. Accurate, fast, DIY-friendly.


Concrete Calculator – Slab Volume & Bag Estimator (m³, ft³):

Use this free concrete slab calculator to estimate concrete volume, m³/ft³, and number of bags for patios, driveways, shed bases and more.

Concrete Slab Calculator

Waste factor: 10% extra
Results

Enter length, width and thickness to calculate your concrete volume.

Approximate yields based on typical ready-mix bags. Always confirm with your supplier and local building codes before ordering materials.

Related Calculators

  • Gravel Calculator
  • Asphalt Calculator
  • Fence Post Calculator
  • Brick Wall Calculator
  • Tile Calculator

How Concrete Volume Is Calculated

Concrete volume is based on a simple formula:

Volume = Length × Width × Depth

Example:
A slab 3m long, 2m wide, and 0.1m thick:
3 × 2 × 0.1 = 0.6 m³


This estimated volume is used to determine how many ready-mix bags you need for your project.

Standard Thickness Guide

  • Patio: 75–100 mm
  • Shed base: 100 mm
  • Driveway (car use): 100–150 mm


Always follow local building codes and supplier guidelines.

Tips for DIY Results

  • Always add 5–10% extra for waste and uneven ground.
  • Check if reinforcement mesh is needed for load areas.
  • Cure concrete slowly to avoid cracks (keep covered or hydrated).

FAQs

Do I need reinforcement?
For driveways, parking, or heavy loads, reinforcement is recommended.

How many bags do I need?
When the live calculator is enabled, it will show the exact number of 25 kg bags required.

How accurate is the estimate?
It provides standard estimation; always verify with a qualified builder or supplier.

Can I calculate in feet and meters?
Yes, once live, the calculator will allow m/ft selection and show both m³ and ft³ results.

pages.title: Paint Calculator
pages.body_html:
Home

Estimate paint required for any room. Calculates liters needed by area and coats. Simple DIY paint calculator.

PAINT CALCULATOR – COVERAGE & LITERS/GALLONS ESTIMATOR (m², ft²)


Use this free paint calculator to estimate how much paint you need for walls, rooms, and home DIY projects. Enter wall dimensions, doors, windows, and number of coats to get instant coverage and total liters/gallons required.

Paint Coverage Calculator

Units:
meters
meters
meters (floor to ceiling)
Most walls use 2 coats.
m² of doors to subtract
m² of windows to subtract
Typical interior paint covers ~10 m² per liter.

Results will appear here once you enter your dimensions.

Approximate coverage only. Always check the can label for exact coverage and round up to the next full can size.

Related Calculators

  • Tile Calculator
  • Flooring Calculator
  • Drywall Calculator
  • Concrete Calculator
  • Brick Wall Calculator

Standard Paint Coverage

Most wall paint covers approximately:
10–12 m² per liter per coat

This varies by:

  • surface texture
  • paint brand and finish
  • roller or spray method

Example

Room: 4m × 3m, with 2.5m wall height
Wall surface: 4×2.5 × 2 (long walls) + 3×2.5 × 2 (short walls)
= 20 + 15
= 35 m² approx.

If you apply 2 coats:
35 m² × 2 = 70 m² total coverage needed

At ~10 m² per liter:
70 ÷ 10 = 7 liters of paint

When to Add Extra

  • Rough or textured walls require more paint
  • Very dark-to-light color changes need a primer or 3rd coat
  • Bathrooms and high-humidity zones benefit from mold-resistant finish

FAQs

How many coats should I use?
Most interior walls need 2 coats for proper finish.

Can I include ceilings?
Yes, once live you can input ceiling area separately.

What about primer?
Primer is recommended if painting new drywall or switching from dark to light color.

Does paint brand matter?
Higher-quality brands provide better coverage per liter, reducing total paint needed.

pages.title: Tile Calculator
pages.body_html:
Home

Calculate tiles needed for floors or walls. Includes waste percentage and box estimate for accurate DIY planning.

Tile Calculator | Boxes & Waste Estimate

This tile calculator helps estimate how many tiles and boxes you need for any flooring or wall tiling project. When activated, the calculator will allow you to enter total area, tile size, and waste percentage to get an accurate estimate.

Tile Quantity Calculator

Units:
meters
meters
centimeters
centimeters
Most jobs use 5–15% extra for cuts and breakage.
Enter if you want to estimate number of boxes.

Enter floor size and tile size to see how many tiles you need.

This is an approximate calculation. Always check the tile box coverage and add extra for pattern matching and future repairs.

Related Calculators

  • Paint Calculator
  • Flooring Calculator
  • Concrete Calculator
  • Gravel Calculator
  • Brick Wall Calculator

Why Waste Percentage Matters

Tiles break, edges get cut, and corners require trims. That is why professional tile installers always add 5–15% extra depending on layout complexity.

  • Straight lay: 5–7% waste
  • Diagonal layout: 10–12%
  • Herringbone/complex patterns: 12–15%

Example

Room size: 4m × 3m = 12 m²
Tile size coverage per tile: 0.04 m² (e.g., 20cm × 20cm)

12 ÷ 0.04 = 300 tiles baseline

If waste is 10%:
300 × 1.10 = 330 tiles

If each box contains 25 tiles:
330 ÷ 25 = 13.2 → 14 boxes

When to Increase Waste

  • Patterned tiles that need directional alignment
  • Small tiles with many cuts
  • Narrow bathrooms with corners and pipes
  • Tiles prone to chipping or cracking during cuts

Tiles Per Box

Always check:

  • Coverage per box on the packaging
  • Whether grout lines affect total layout area

Box coverage typically ranges between 1–1.5 m² per box.

FAQs

How much extra should I buy?
Minimum 5% for straight layouts, up to 15% for patterned layouts.

Does grout change tile count?
Grout lines don’t change area coverage enough to impact total calculation significantly.

Can I calculate for walls and floors?
Yes, the full calculator will support both.

What if tiles go out of stock?
Buying extra prevents mismatched batches or tone differences later.

pages.title: Gravel Calculator
pages.body_html:
Home

Estimate gravel needed for driveways, garden beds, and pathways. Calculate volume and tons required.

Gravel Volume Calculator | Driveway & Garden Fill

Use this gravel calculator to estimate how much gravel you need for any landscaping, driveway, or ground-fill project. Once activated, you will be able to enter total length, width, and depth to receive an instant volume calculation in cubic meters and tons.

Gravel Volume & Weight Calculator

Units:
meters
meters
centimeters
Commonly 5–15% extra for compaction and uneven ground.
Typical gravel density is 1.5–1.7 tonnes per cubic meter.

Enter area size and depth to estimate gravel volume and weight.

This is an approximate calculation. Always check with your supplier which density and delivery unit (tonnes, cubic yards, etc.) they use.

Related Calculators

  • Concrete Calculator
  • Asphalt Calculator
  • Fence Post Calculator
  • Roofing Calculator
  • Brick Wall Calculator

Standard Gravel Depth Guidelines

  • Paths & walkways: 50–75 mm
  • Driveways: 100–150 mm
  • Drainage layers: 150–200 mm

Example

Driveway: 6m × 3m with depth 0.1m

Volume = 6 × 3 × 0.1 = 1.8 m³

If gravel density is ~1.6 tons per m³:
1.8 × 1.6 = 2.88 tons required

Gravel Density

Most gravel types range between 1.5–1.8 tons per m³, depending on:

  • stone type
  • moisture level
  • compaction rate

Tips Before Ordering Gravel

  • Consider compaction: final level settles lower
  • Add 5–10% extra for delivery loss or leveling
  • Ensure weed membrane beneath for clean finish
  • Avoid spreading in heavy rain; water bloats weight

Gravel Types

  • Pea gravel: smooth, decorative
  • Crushed gravel: structural, driveways
  • River rock: larger, landscaping
  • Limestone/granite: compact well, ideal load-bearing

FAQs

How accurate is gravel volume estimation?
It provides standard density-based calculation and is generally accurate for most DIY uses.

Can I convert m³ to tons?
Yes, the live calculator will show both.

Should I add sand beneath gravel?
For driveways or paths, a compacted base layer is recommended.

pages.title: Drywall Calculator
pages.body_html:
Home

Calculate how many drywall sheets you need for walls and ceilings. Includes waste and screw estimates.

Drywall Sheet Calculator | Room & Wall Coverage

This drywall calculator estimates how many sheets are needed for any room, wall, or ceiling installation. When the full tool is activated, you will be able to enter wall dimensions and automatically get sheet count, waste, and screw estimates.

Drywall Sheet Calculator

Units:
meters
meters
meters (floor to ceiling)
Include ceiling if you are boarding the full room.
Choose common board size or set custom.
Use 10–15% for offcuts, openings and mistakes.
meters or feet, based on units
meters or feet, based on units

Enter room size and sheet size to estimate drywall sheets.

This calculator assumes standard rectangular rooms. Irregular shapes, stairwells and complex layouts should be measured separately or checked by a contractor.

Related Calculators

  • Paint Calculator
  • Flooring Calculator
  • Tile Calculator
  • Concrete Calculator
  • Brick Wall Calculator

Standard Sheet Sizes

Common drywall sizes:

  • 1200 × 2400 mm
  • 1200 × 3000 mm
  • 1200 × 3600 mm

Area coverage per sheet:

  • 2.88 m² (standard 1200 × 2400)
  • 3.60 m² (1200 × 3000)
  • 4.32 m² (1200 × 3600)

Example

Room perimeter area: 45 m²
Standard sheet coverage: 2.88 m²

45 ÷ 2.88 ≈ 15.6 → 16 sheets

Add 10% waste:
16 × 1.10 ≈ 18 sheets

When to Add Waste

  • Multiple cuts needed for windows and doors
  • Ceiling mounts where board trimming is required
  • Non-square rooms

Screw Estimate (Approx.)

A common drywall estimate:

  • 50–60 screws per 1200 × 2400mm sheet
  • Ceilings require more due to gravity pull

Moisture-Resistant Use

Use green or blue boards for:

  • Bathrooms
  • Kitchens
  • Laundry rooms
  • Basements

Standard boards are fine for living and bedroom spaces.

FAQs

How much waste should I include?
10% for simple layouts, 12–15% for rooms with multiple openings.

Can I mix board sizes?
Yes, mixing can reduce waste and seams.

Do ceilings need different board types?
Ceilings can use thicker board to prevent sagging.

pages.title: Roofing Calculator
pages.body_html:
Home

Estimate roofing shingles bundles needed based on roof slope, area, and waste percentage.

Roofing Shingles Calculator | Bundles & Waste Estimate

Use this roofing shingles calculator to estimate how many bundles of shingles are required for full roof coverage. Once activated, you will be able to enter roof area, slope, and waste percentage to get a complete estimate.

Roofing Shingle Calculator

Units:
meters along the ridge
meters across the building
Degrees (e.g. 25–45° typical). Leave blank to ignore slope.
Commonly 10–15% extra for cuts, hips and valleys.
Metric: m² per bundle (typical 2.5–3 m²). Imperial: bundles per roofing square (~3).

Enter building size and slope to estimate roofing shingles.

This tool assumes a simple gable roof with two equal slopes. Complex roofs (hips, dormers, multiple sections) should be measured section by section or checked by a roofing professional.

Related Calculators

  • Asphalt Calculator
  • Gravel Calculator
  • Concrete Calculator
  • Paint Calculator
  • Tile Calculator

Shingle Coverage Basics

One bundle of asphalt shingles covers approximately:
3.0 to 3.5 m² depending on brand and style.

Roof area increases with slope:

  • Low slope (10–20°): +5% material
  • Mid slope (20–30°): +10%
  • Steep slope (30°+): +12–15%

Example

Roof area (flat): 100 m²
If slope is mid (25°): add 10%
100 × 1.10 = 110 m² adjusted area

If each bundle covers 3.3 m²:
110 ÷ 3.3 ≈ 33 bundles

Waste Percentage

Standard waste:

  • Straight layout: 8–10%
  • Valleys/edges: 12–15%
  • Complex roof geometry: 15–18%

When to Add Extra

  • Valleys and multiple pitch lines
  • Skylights and chimneys
  • Patterned shingles

Ventilation Note

Roof lifespan depends heavily on attic ventilation and underlayment integrity.

FAQs

How accurate is shingle estimation?
For standard roof shapes and slopes, estimates are typically reliable within ±10%.

Do different shingles vary in coverage?
Yes, always check manufacturer coverage per bundle.

What about hip and ridge caps?
These require additional bundles, typically listed separately on packaging.

pages.title: Flooring Calculator
pages.body_html:
Home

Calculate flooring boxes needed based on room area and waste percentage. Simple estimate for laminate, parquet, and vinyl.

Flooring Calculator | Boxes & Waste Estimate

This flooring calculator estimates how many boxes of laminate, vinyl, or parquet flooring you need for any room. When activated, you will be able to enter total room area and waste percentage to receive an accurate box count.

Flooring / Parquet Calculator

Units:
meters
meters
Extra m² or ft² for closets, niches, etc.
Most flooring uses 5–10% extra for cuts and pattern.
Metric: m² per box (often 1.5–2.5 m²). Imperial: ft² per box.

Enter room size to estimate flooring area and boxes.

Check the exact coverage printed on your flooring or parquet packaging and always round up to full boxes.

Related Calculators

  • Tile Calculator
  • Paint Calculator
  • Drywall Calculator
  • Concrete Calculator
  • Brick Wall Calculator

Standard Coverage

Flooring boxes typically cover:

  • 1.8 to 2.3 m² per box (varies by brand and plank size)

Always check package coverage printed on the box before final purchase.

Waste Percentage

Standard waste:

  • Straight plank layout: 5–7%
  • Diagonal layout: 10–12%
  • Herringbone/complex patterns: 12–15%

Example

Room size: 5m × 4m = 20 m²
Waste: 10%
20 × 1.10 = 22 m² total coverage

If each box covers 2.0 m²:
22 ÷ 2.0 = 11 boxes needed

Thickness & Wear Layer

Flooring durability depends on:

  • Thickness (e.g. 8mm, 10mm, 12mm)
  • Wear layer rating (e.g. AC3, AC4, AC5)

Higher AC rating = better resistance to scratches and foot traffic.

Subfloor Preparation

  • Ensure level surface
  • Use moisture barrier if installing over concrete slab
  • Allow acclimatization of planks (24–72 hours in room)

FAQs

How much waste should I add?
Minimum 5% for simple plank installs, 12–15% for patterned layouts.

Does plank thickness change quantity?
No, thickness affects durability, not area coverage.

Can I mix box types?
Avoid mixing batches due to tone variation.

pages.title: Asphalt Calculator
pages.body_html:
Home

Estimate asphalt required for driveways and surfaces. Calculates tons based on area and depth.

Asphalt Volume Calculator | Driveway Tonnage Estimate

This asphalt calculator estimates how much asphalt you need for driveway paving or surface restoration. The full calculator will allow you to enter area and depth, returning both cubic meters and tons.

Asphalt Volume & Tonnage Calculator

Units:
meters
meters
centimeters (typical 5–10 cm)
Commonly 5–10% extra for loss and compaction.
Typical hot mix density is around 2.3–2.5 tonnes per cubic meter.

Enter area size and thickness to estimate asphalt volume and weight.

This is an approximate estimate. Always confirm thickness, density and truck load size with your asphalt supplier or contractor.

Related Calculators

  • Gravel Calculator
  • Roofing Calculator
  • Concrete Calculator
  • Fence Post Calculator
  • Brick Wall Calculator

Standard Asphalt Thickness

Apply depth based on vehicle usage:

  • Light car driveway: 50–75 mm
  • Multi-vehicle/heavy use: 75–100 mm

Density Reference

Standard hot-mix asphalt density:
2.3–2.4 tons per cubic meter

Example

Driveway: 9m × 3m, depth 0.075m (75mm)

Volume = 9 × 3 × 0.075 = 2.025 m³
Weight:
2.025 × 2.35 (average density) ≈ 4.75 tons

Base Layer

A compacted base layer is recommended:

  • Crushed stone or gravel base
  • 100–150 mm thickness

This prevents cracking and early sink points.

Weather Considerations

  • Avoid installation below 10°C
  • Rain affects compaction and adhesion
  • Hot mix cools fast: laying efficiency matters

FAQs

Are asphalt tons estimates accurate?
Yes, for standard mixes ±5% variation.

Can I convert to square meters?
Surface area is measured in square meters, volume in cubic meters, tons are based on density.

Do I need a base layer?
Yes, most driveway failures come from weak base preparation.

pages.title: Fence Post Calculator
pages.body_html:
Home

Estimate concrete needed per fence post, including depth and hole diameter. Simple DIY post footing calculation.

Fence Post Concrete Calculator | Bags per Post

This fence post calculator estimates how many 25 kg concrete bags are required per post. Once activated, you will enter post spacing, hole diameter, and depth to determine concrete volume and bag requirements.

Fence Post Concrete Calculator

Units:
meters
meters between posts
centimeters
centimeters
For overdigging, bell-shaped holes, loss, etc.
Metric: m³ per bag (~0.02 m³ for 25 kg). Imperial: ft³ per bag (~0.6 ft³).

Enter fence length, spacing and hole size to estimate concrete.

This is an approximate estimate based on cylindrical post holes. Always check local frost depth and post size with your builder or supplier.

Related Calculators

  • Concrete Calculator
  • Gravel Calculator
  • Asphalt Calculator
  • Roofing Calculator
  • Brick Wall Calculator

Standard Post Hole Dimensions

General guideline:

  • Hole diameter: 2–3× post width
  • Depth: 1/3 of total post height (minimum)

Example:
Post height: 2.1m
Depth: 2.1 ÷ 3 ≈ 0.70m

Volume Example

Hole diameter: 300mm (0.3m)
Hole depth: 0.70m

Volume = π × (0.15²) × 0.70
≈ 0.049 m³ per post

Bag conversion:
1 m³ ≈ 1000L and 25kg bag covers ≈ 10L

0.049 m³ = 49L
49 ÷ 10 ≈ 5 bags per post

Concrete Mix Tip

Fast-setting post concrete is recommended for:

  • Fences
  • Mailboxes
  • Garden structures

Standard mix is fine but requires bracing and longer set time.

Frequently Asked Questions

How many bags per post on average?
Typically 3–6 bags depending on post size and depth.

Should I use gravel at the bottom?
A 100mm gravel base improves drainage.

Do posts need bracing?
Yes, brace during set time to avoid lean or shift.

pages.title: Brick Calculator
pages.body_html:
Home

Calculate brick count and mortar volume needed for walls. Includes standard brick sizes and waste percentage.

Brick Wall Calculator | Bricks & Mortar Estimate

Use this brick wall calculator to estimate bricks and mortar needed for full wall construction or repairs. When live, you will enter wall length, height, and brick dimensions to get results.

Brick Wall Calculator

Units:
meters
meters
Typical single-skin wall: ~60 bricks per m² (including joints).
Use 5–10% extra for cuts and breakage.
Enter to estimate pallets or packs needed.

Enter wall size to estimate bricks needed.

This calculator assumes a single-skin brick wall with standard mortar joints. For cavity walls, double-skin walls or complex openings, calculate each section separately or consult a mason.

Standard Brick Sizes

Most bricks:

  • 215 × 102.5 × 65 mm

Coverage:

  • ~60 bricks per m² including mortar joints

Example

Wall: 4m × 2.5m = 10 m²
Bricks:
10 × 60 ≈ 600 bricks

Waste:
600 × 1.05 ≈ 630 bricks (5% waste)

Mortar Estimate

Standard mortar:

  • ~0.03 m³ per m² of brickwork

Example:
10 m² × 0.03 = 0.30 m³ mortar

When to Increase Waste

  • Decorative patterns
  • Multiple window/door openings
  • Uneven substrate

FAQs

How many bricks per m²?
Approximately 60 bricks including joints.

What is mortar coverage per m²?
About 0.03 m³ per m² of wall.

Does brick orientation change count?
Patterns like basketweave or herringbone increase waste.

pages.body_html:

Legal & Privacy Notice

Last Updated: December 2025

This website (“Material Calc Pro”) provides free construction and DIY calculators for informational purposes.

1. Data Collection & Cookies

We do not ask users to create accounts or submit personal information.

However, like most websites, basic technical data may be collected automatically, such as:

  • IP address
  • device and browser type
  • pages visited and duration
  • general location (city/country level)

We may use cookies and similar technologies to maintain site functionality, improve performance, and measure traffic.

If you are located in the EU/EEA or another region requiring consent, a cookie notice may appear when you first access the site.

2. Advertising & Google AdSense

This website may display ads provided by Google AdSense and its third-party advertising partners.

Ads may use cookies to:

  • deliver personalized or non-personalized ads
  • measure ad performance
  • prevent fraud and abuse

You can manage ad personalization through Google settings.

We do not control Google’s cookie usage and do not sell user data.

3. Analytics & Server Logs

We may use basic analytics tools (including Google Analytics or similar) to understand site usage.

Hosting services may generate server logs automatically.

This information is used only to maintain and improve website performance.

We do not identify users personally and do not sell or share this data.

4. Results Disclaimer (Very Important)

All calculators on this website provide estimates only.

Material quantities and construction needs vary due to:

  • installation method
  • waste factors
  • local building codes
  • material density differences
  • soil / slope / weather conditions
  • professional judgment

Always verify with a licensed contractor, builder, or engineer before purchasing materials or beginning work.

We do not provide engineering or architectural advice.

5. No Liability

To the maximum extent permitted by law, we are not responsible for:

  • project errors or material miscalculations
  • damages, loss, or cost arising from use of calculator estimates
  • technical issues, outages, or incorrect data

Use this website at your own discretion and risk.

6. External Links

We may include links to third-party sites.

We do not control or endorse their content, policies, or security practices.

7. Children’s Information

This site is not directed toward children under 13.

We do not knowingly collect data from minors.

8. Contact

For data inquiries, correction, removal, calculator issues, or any other questions:

Email: info@catalitium.com

9. Updates

This page may be updated occasionally to reflect changes in advertising, law, or site functionality.

Continued use of the site after updates indicates acceptance.

Use this free concrete slab calculator to estimate concrete volume, m³/ft³, and number of bags for patios, driveways, shed bases and more.

calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Concrete Slab Calculator

Waste factor: 10% extra
Results

Enter length, width and thickness to calculate your concrete volume.

Approximate yields based on typical ready-mix bags. Always confirm with your supplier and local building codes before ordering materials.

calculator_content_blocks.heading: How Concrete Volume Is Calculated
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
How Concrete Volume Is Calculated

Concrete volume is based on a simple formula:

Volume = Length × Width × Depth

Example:
A slab 3m long, 2m wide, and 0.1m thick:
3 × 2 × 0.1 = 0.6 m³


This estimated volume is used to determine how many ready-mix bags you need for your project.

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Standard Thickness Guide
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Standard Thickness Guide

  • Patio: 75–100 mm
  • Shed base: 100 mm
  • Driveway (car use): 100–150 mm


Always follow local building codes and supplier guidelines.

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Tips for DIY Results
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Tips for DIY Results

  • Always add 5–10% extra for waste and uneven ground.
  • Check if reinforcement mesh is needed for load areas.
  • Cure concrete slowly to avoid cracks (keep covered or hydrated).

calculator_content_blocks.heading: FAQs
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
FAQs

Do I need reinforcement?
For driveways, parking, or heavy loads, reinforcement is recommended.

How many bags do I need?
When the live calculator is enabled, it will show the exact number of 25 kg bags required.

How accurate is the estimate?
It provides standard estimation; always verify with a qualified builder or supplier.

Can I calculate in feet and meters?
Yes, once live, the calculator will allow m/ft selection and show both m³ and ft³ results.

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Standard Paint Coverage
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Standard Paint Coverage

Most wall paint covers approximately:
10–12 m² per liter per coat

This varies by:

  • surface texture
  • paint brand and finish
  • roller or spray method

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Example
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Example

Room: 4m × 3m, with 2.5m wall height
Wall surface: 4×2.5 × 2 (long walls) + 3×2.5 × 2 (short walls)
= 20 + 15
= 35 m² approx.

If you apply 2 coats:
35 m² × 2 = 70 m² total coverage needed

At ~10 m² per liter:
70 ÷ 10 = 7 liters of paint

calculator_content_blocks.heading: When to Add Extra
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
When to Add Extra

  • Rough or textured walls require more paint
  • Very dark-to-light color changes need a primer or 3rd coat
  • Bathrooms and high-humidity zones benefit from mold-resistant finish

calculator_content_blocks.heading: FAQs
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
FAQs

How many coats should I use?
Most interior walls need 2 coats for proper finish.

Can I include ceilings?
Yes, once live you can input ceiling area separately.

What about primer?
Primer is recommended if painting new drywall or switching from dark to light color.

Does paint brand matter?
Higher-quality brands provide better coverage per liter, reducing total paint needed.

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Tile Quantity Calculator
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Tile Quantity Calculator

Units:
meters
meters
centimeters
centimeters
Most jobs use 5–15% extra for cuts and breakage.
Enter if you want to estimate number of boxes.

Enter floor size and tile size to see how many tiles you need.

This is an approximate calculation. Always check the tile box coverage and add extra for pattern matching and future repairs.

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Why Waste Percentage Matters
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Why Waste Percentage Matters

Tiles break, edges get cut, and corners require trims. That is why professional tile installers always add 5–15% extra depending on layout complexity.

  • Straight lay: 5–7% waste
  • Diagonal layout: 10–12%
  • Herringbone/complex patterns: 12–15%

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Example
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Example

Room size: 4m × 3m = 12 m²
Tile size coverage per tile: 0.04 m² (e.g., 20cm × 20cm)

12 ÷ 0.04 = 300 tiles baseline

If waste is 10%:
300 × 1.10 = 330 tiles

If each box contains 25 tiles:
330 ÷ 25 = 13.2 → 14 boxes

calculator_content_blocks.heading: When to Increase Waste
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
When to Increase Waste

  • Patterned tiles that need directional alignment
  • Small tiles with many cuts
  • Narrow bathrooms with corners and pipes
  • Tiles prone to chipping or cracking during cuts

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Tiles Per Box
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Tiles Per Box

Always check:

  • Coverage per box on the packaging
  • Whether grout lines affect total layout area

Box coverage typically ranges between 1–1.5 m² per box.

calculator_content_blocks.heading: FAQs
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
FAQs

How much extra should I buy?
Minimum 5% for straight layouts, up to 15% for patterned layouts.

Does grout change tile count?
Grout lines don’t change area coverage enough to impact total calculation significantly.

Can I calculate for walls and floors?
Yes, the full calculator will support both.

What if tiles go out of stock?
Buying extra prevents mismatched batches or tone differences later.

calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Gravel Volume & Weight Calculator

meters
meters
centimeters
Commonly 5–15% extra for compaction and uneven ground.
Typical gravel density is 1.5–1.7 tonnes per cubic meter.

Enter area size and depth to estimate gravel volume and weight.

This is an approximate calculation. Always check with your supplier which density and delivery unit (tonnes, cubic yards, etc.) they use.

  • Concrete Calculator

  • Roofing Calculator

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Standard Gravel Depth Guidelines
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Standard Gravel Depth Guidelines

  • Paths & walkways: 50–75 mm
  • Driveways: 100–150 mm
  • Drainage layers: 150–200 mm

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Example
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Example

Driveway: 6m × 3m with depth 0.1m

Volume = 6 × 3 × 0.1 = 1.8 m³

If gravel density is ~1.6 tons per m³:
1.8 × 1.6 = 2.88 tons required

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Gravel Density
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Gravel Density

Most gravel types range between 1.5–1.8 tons per m³, depending on:

  • stone type
  • moisture level
  • compaction rate

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Tips Before Ordering Gravel
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Tips Before Ordering Gravel

  • Consider compaction: final level settles lower
  • Add 5–10% extra for delivery loss or leveling
  • Ensure weed membrane beneath for clean finish
  • Avoid spreading in heavy rain; water bloats weight

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Gravel Types
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Gravel Types

  • Pea gravel: smooth, decorative
  • Crushed gravel: structural, driveways
  • River rock: larger, landscaping
  • Limestone/granite: compact well, ideal load-bearing

calculator_content_blocks.heading: FAQs
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
FAQs

How accurate is gravel volume estimation?
It provides standard density-based calculation and is generally accurate for most DIY uses.

Can I convert m³ to tons?
Yes, the live calculator will show both.

Should I add sand beneath gravel?
For driveways or paths, a compacted base layer is recommended.

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Standard Sheet Sizes
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Standard Sheet Sizes

Common drywall sizes:

  • 1200 × 2400 mm
  • 1200 × 3000 mm
  • 1200 × 3600 mm

Area coverage per sheet:

  • 2.88 m² (standard 1200 × 2400)
  • 3.60 m² (1200 × 3000)
  • 4.32 m² (1200 × 3600)

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Example
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Example

Room perimeter area: 45 m²
Standard sheet coverage: 2.88 m²

45 ÷ 2.88 ≈ 15.6 → 16 sheets

Add 10% waste:
16 × 1.10 ≈ 18 sheets

calculator_content_blocks.heading: When to Add Waste
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
When to Add Waste

  • Multiple cuts needed for windows and doors
  • Ceiling mounts where board trimming is required
  • Non-square rooms

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Screw Estimate (Approx.)
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Screw Estimate (Approx.)

A common drywall estimate:

  • 50–60 screws per 1200 × 2400mm sheet
  • Ceilings require more due to gravity pull

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Moisture-Resistant Use
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Moisture-Resistant Use

Use green or blue boards for:

  • Bathrooms
  • Kitchens
  • Laundry rooms
  • Basements

Standard boards are fine for living and bedroom spaces.

calculator_content_blocks.heading: FAQs
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
FAQs

How much waste should I include?
10% for simple layouts, 12–15% for rooms with multiple openings.

Can I mix board sizes?
Yes, mixing can reduce waste and seams.

Do ceilings need different board types?
Ceilings can use thicker board to prevent sagging.

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Shingle Coverage Basics
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Shingle Coverage Basics

One bundle of asphalt shingles covers approximately:
3.0 to 3.5 m² depending on brand and style.

Roof area increases with slope:

  • Low slope (10–20°): +5% material
  • Mid slope (20–30°): +10%
  • Steep slope (30°+): +12–15%

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Example
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Example

Roof area (flat): 100 m²
If slope is mid (25°): add 10%
100 × 1.10 = 110 m² adjusted area

If each bundle covers 3.3 m²:
110 ÷ 3.3 ≈ 33 bundles

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Waste Percentage
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Waste Percentage

Standard waste:

  • Straight layout: 8–10%
  • Valleys/edges: 12–15%
  • Complex roof geometry: 15–18%

calculator_content_blocks.heading: When to Add Extra
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
When to Add Extra

  • Valleys and multiple pitch lines
  • Skylights and chimneys
  • Patterned shingles

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Ventilation Note
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Ventilation Note

Roof lifespan depends heavily on attic ventilation and underlayment integrity.

calculator_content_blocks.heading: FAQs
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
FAQs

How accurate is shingle estimation?
For standard roof shapes and slopes, estimates are typically reliable within ±10%.

Do different shingles vary in coverage?
Yes, always check manufacturer coverage per bundle.

What about hip and ridge caps?
These require additional bundles, typically listed separately on packaging.

calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Flooring Calculator | Boxes & Waste Estimate

This flooring calculator estimates how many boxes of laminate, vinyl, or parquet flooring you need for any room. When activated, you will be able to enter total room area and waste percentage to receive an accurate box count.

calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Flooring / Parquet Calculator

Units:
meters
meters
Extra m² or ft² for closets, niches, etc.
Most flooring uses 5–10% extra for cuts and pattern.
Metric: m² per box (often 1.5–2.5 m²). Imperial: ft² per box.

Enter room size to estimate flooring area and boxes.

Check the exact coverage printed on your flooring or parquet packaging and always round up to full boxes.

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Standard Coverage
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Standard Coverage

Flooring boxes typically cover:

  • 1.8 to 2.3 m² per box (varies by brand and plank size)

Always check package coverage printed on the box before final purchase.

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Waste Percentage
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Waste Percentage

Standard waste:

  • Straight plank layout: 5–7%
  • Diagonal layout: 10–12%
  • Herringbone/complex patterns: 12–15%

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Example
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Example

Room size: 5m × 4m = 20 m²
Waste: 10%
20 × 1.10 = 22 m² total coverage

If each box covers 2.0 m²:
22 ÷ 2.0 = 11 boxes needed

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Thickness & Wear Layer
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Thickness & Wear Layer

Flooring durability depends on:

  • Thickness (e.g. 8mm, 10mm, 12mm)
  • Wear layer rating (e.g. AC3, AC4, AC5)

Higher AC rating = better resistance to scratches and foot traffic.

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Subfloor Preparation
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Subfloor Preparation

  • Ensure level surface
  • Use moisture barrier if installing over concrete slab
  • Allow acclimatization of planks (24–72 hours in room)

calculator_content_blocks.heading: FAQs
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
FAQs

How much waste should I add?
Minimum 5% for simple plank installs, 12–15% for patterned layouts.

Does plank thickness change quantity?
No, thickness affects durability, not area coverage.

Can I mix box types?
Avoid mixing batches due to tone variation.

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Standard Asphalt Thickness
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Standard Asphalt Thickness

Apply depth based on vehicle usage:

  • Light car driveway: 50–75 mm
  • Multi-vehicle/heavy use: 75–100 mm

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Density Reference
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Density Reference

Standard hot-mix asphalt density:
2.3–2.4 tons per cubic meter

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Example
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Example

Driveway: 9m × 3m, depth 0.075m (75mm)

Volume = 9 × 3 × 0.075 = 2.025 m³
Weight:
2.025 × 2.35 (average density) ≈ 4.75 tons

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Base Layer
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Base Layer

A compacted base layer is recommended:

  • Crushed stone or gravel base
  • 100–150 mm thickness

This prevents cracking and early sink points.

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Weather Considerations
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Weather Considerations

  • Avoid installation below 10°C
  • Rain affects compaction and adhesion
  • Hot mix cools fast: laying efficiency matters

calculator_content_blocks.heading: FAQs
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
FAQs

Are asphalt tons estimates accurate?
Yes, for standard mixes ±5% variation.

Can I convert to square meters?
Surface area is measured in square meters, volume in cubic meters, tons are based on density.

Do I need a base layer?
Yes, most driveway failures come from weak base preparation.

For overdigging, bell-shaped holes, loss, etc.
Metric: m³ per bag (~0.02 m³ for 25 kg). Imperial: ft³ per bag (~0.6 ft³).

Enter fence length, spacing and hole size to estimate concrete.

This is an approximate estimate based on cylindrical post holes. Always check local frost depth and post size with your builder or supplier.

Related Calculators

  • Concrete Calculator
  • Gravel Calculator
  • Asphalt Calculator
  • Roofing Calculator
  • Brick Wall Calculator

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Standard Post Hole Dimensions
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Standard Post Hole Dimensions

General guideline:

  • Hole diameter: 2–3× post width
  • Depth: 1/3 of total post height (minimum)

Example:
Post height: 2.1m
Depth: 2.1 ÷ 3 ≈ 0.70m

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Volume Example
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Volume Example

Hole diameter: 300mm (0.3m)
Hole depth: 0.70m

Volume = π × (0.15²) × 0.70
≈ 0.049 m³ per post

Bag conversion:
1 m³ ≈ 1000L and 25kg bag covers ≈ 10L

0.049 m³ = 49L
49 ÷ 10 ≈ 5 bags per post

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Concrete Mix Tip
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Concrete Mix Tip

Fast-setting post concrete is recommended for:

  • Fences
  • Mailboxes
  • Garden structures

Standard mix is fine but requires bracing and longer set time.

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Frequently Asked Questions
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Frequently Asked Questions

How many bags per post on average?
Typically 3–6 bags depending on post size and depth.

Should I use gravel at the bottom?
A 100mm gravel base improves drainage.

Do posts need bracing?
Yes, brace during set time to avoid lean or shift.

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Brick Wall Calculator | Bricks & Mortar Estimate
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Brick Wall Calculator | Bricks & Mortar Estimate

Use this brick wall calculator to estimate bricks and mortar needed for full wall construction or repairs. When live, you will enter wall length, height, and brick dimensions to get results.

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Brick Wall Calculator
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Brick Wall Calculator

Units:
meters
meters
Typical single-skin wall: ~60 bricks per m² (including joints).
Use 5–10% extra for cuts and breakage.
Enter to estimate pallets or packs needed.

Enter wall size to estimate bricks needed.

This calculator assumes a single-skin brick wall with standard mortar joints. For cavity walls, double-skin walls or complex openings, calculate each section separately or consult a mason.

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Standard Brick Sizes
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Standard Brick Sizes

Most bricks:

  • 215 × 102.5 × 65 mm

Coverage:

  • ~60 bricks per m² including mortar joints

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Example
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Example

Wall: 4m × 2.5m = 10 m²
Bricks:
10 × 60 ≈ 600 bricks

Waste:
600 × 1.05 ≈ 630 bricks (5% waste)

calculator_content_blocks.heading: Mortar Estimate
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
Mortar Estimate

Standard mortar:

  • ~0.03 m³ per m² of brickwork

Example:
10 m² × 0.03 = 0.30 m³ mortar

calculator_content_blocks.heading: When to Increase Waste
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
When to Increase Waste

  • Decorative patterns
  • Multiple window/door openings
  • Uneven substrate

calculator_content_blocks.heading: FAQs
calculator_content_blocks.content_html:
FAQs

How many bricks per m²?
Approximately 60 bricks including joints.

What is mortar coverage per m²?
About 0.03 m³ per m² of wall.

Does brick orientation change count?
Patterns like basketweave or herringbone increase waste.

source_payload.title: Concrete Calculator
source_payload.contentHtml:
Home

Instant concrete volume and bag estimate calculator for slabs and small projects. Accurate, fast, DIY-friendly.


Concrete Calculator – Slab Volume & Bag Estimator (m³, ft³):

Use this free concrete slab calculator to estimate concrete volume, m³/ft³, and number of bags for patios, driveways, shed bases and more.

Concrete Slab Calculator

Waste factor: 10% extra
Results

Enter length, width and thickness to calculate your concrete volume.

Approximate yields based on typical ready-mix bags. Always confirm with your supplier and local building codes before ordering materials.

Related Calculators

  • Gravel Calculator
  • Asphalt Calculator
  • Fence Post Calculator
  • Brick Wall Calculator
  • Tile Calculator

How Concrete Volume Is Calculated

Concrete volume is based on a simple formula:

Volume = Length × Width × Depth

Example:
A slab 3m long, 2m wide, and 0.1m thick:
3 × 2 × 0.1 = 0.6 m³


This estimated volume is used to determine how many ready-mix bags you need for your project.

Standard Thickness Guide

  • Patio: 75–100 mm
  • Shed base: 100 mm
  • Driveway (car use): 100–150 mm


Always follow local building codes and supplier guidelines.

Tips for DIY Results

  • Always add 5–10% extra for waste and uneven ground.
  • Check if reinforcement mesh is needed for load areas.
  • Cure concrete slowly to avoid cracks (keep covered or hydrated).

FAQs

Do I need reinforcement?
For driveways, parking, or heavy loads, reinforcement is recommended.

How many bags do I need?
When the live calculator is enabled, it will show the exact number of 25 kg bags required.

How accurate is the estimate?
It provides standard estimation; always verify with a qualified builder or supplier.

Can I calculate in feet and meters?
Yes, once live, the calculator will allow m/ft selection and show both m³ and ft³ results.

source_payload.title: Paint Calculator
source_payload.contentHtml:
Home

Estimate paint required for any room. Calculates liters needed by area and coats. Simple DIY paint calculator.

PAINT CALCULATOR – COVERAGE & LITERS/GALLONS ESTIMATOR (m², ft²)


Use this free paint calculator to estimate how much paint you need for walls, rooms, and home DIY projects. Enter wall dimensions, doors, windows, and number of coats to get instant coverage and total liters/gallons required.

Paint Coverage Calculator

Units:
meters
meters
meters (floor to ceiling)
Most walls use 2 coats.
m² of doors to subtract
m² of windows to subtract
Typical interior paint covers ~10 m² per liter.

Results will appear here once you enter your dimensions.

Approximate coverage only. Always check the can label for exact coverage and round up to the next full can size.

Related Calculators

  • Tile Calculator
  • Flooring Calculator
  • Drywall Calculator
  • Concrete Calculator
  • Brick Wall Calculator

Standard Paint Coverage

Most wall paint covers approximately:
10–12 m² per liter per coat

This varies by:

  • surface texture
  • paint brand and finish
  • roller or spray method

Example

Room: 4m × 3m, with 2.5m wall height
Wall surface: 4×2.5 × 2 (long walls) + 3×2.5 × 2 (short walls)
= 20 + 15
= 35 m² approx.

If you apply 2 coats:
35 m² × 2 = 70 m² total coverage needed

At ~10 m² per liter:
70 ÷ 10 = 7 liters of paint

When to Add Extra

  • Rough or textured walls require more paint
  • Very dark-to-light color changes need a primer or 3rd coat
  • Bathrooms and high-humidity zones benefit from mold-resistant finish

FAQs

How many coats should I use?
Most interior walls need 2 coats for proper finish.

Can I include ceilings?
Yes, once live you can input ceiling area separately.

What about primer?
Primer is recommended if painting new drywall or switching from dark to light color.

Does paint brand matter?
Higher-quality brands provide better coverage per liter, reducing total paint needed.

source_payload.title: Tile Calculator
source_payload.contentHtml:
Home

Calculate tiles needed for floors or walls. Includes waste percentage and box estimate for accurate DIY planning.

Tile Calculator | Boxes & Waste Estimate

This tile calculator helps estimate how many tiles and boxes you need for any flooring or wall tiling project. When activated, the calculator will allow you to enter total area, tile size, and waste percentage to get an accurate estimate.

Tile Quantity Calculator

Units:
meters
meters
centimeters
centimeters
Most jobs use 5–15% extra for cuts and breakage.
Enter if you want to estimate number of boxes.

Enter floor size and tile size to see how many tiles you need.

This is an approximate calculation. Always check the tile box coverage and add extra for pattern matching and future repairs.

Related Calculators

  • Paint Calculator
  • Flooring Calculator
  • Concrete Calculator
  • Gravel Calculator
  • Brick Wall Calculator

Why Waste Percentage Matters

Tiles break, edges get cut, and corners require trims. That is why professional tile installers always add 5–15% extra depending on layout complexity.

  • Straight lay: 5–7% waste
  • Diagonal layout: 10–12%
  • Herringbone/complex patterns: 12–15%

Example

Room size: 4m × 3m = 12 m²
Tile size coverage per tile: 0.04 m² (e.g., 20cm × 20cm)

12 ÷ 0.04 = 300 tiles baseline

If waste is 10%:
300 × 1.10 = 330 tiles

If each box contains 25 tiles:
330 ÷ 25 = 13.2 → 14 boxes

When to Increase Waste

  • Patterned tiles that need directional alignment
  • Small tiles with many cuts
  • Narrow bathrooms with corners and pipes
  • Tiles prone to chipping or cracking during cuts

Tiles Per Box

Always check:

  • Coverage per box on the packaging
  • Whether grout lines affect total layout area

Box coverage typically ranges between 1–1.5 m² per box.

FAQs

How much extra should I buy?
Minimum 5% for straight layouts, up to 15% for patterned layouts.

Does grout change tile count?
Grout lines don’t change area coverage enough to impact total calculation significantly.

Can I calculate for walls and floors?
Yes, the full calculator will support both.

What if tiles go out of stock?
Buying extra prevents mismatched batches or tone differences later.

sections.heading: Tile Calculator | Boxes & Waste Estimate
sections.content_html:
Tile Calculator | Boxes & Waste Estimate

This tile calculator helps estimate how many tiles and boxes you need for any flooring or wall tiling project. When activated, the calculator will allow you to enter total area, tile size, and waste percentage to get an accurate estimate.

sections.heading: Tile Quantity Calculator
sections.content_html:
Tile Quantity Calculator

Units:
meters
meters
centimeters
centimeters
Most jobs use 5–15% extra for cuts and breakage.
Enter if you want to estimate number of boxes.

Enter floor size and tile size to see how many tiles you need.

This is an approximate calculation. Always check the tile box coverage and add extra for pattern matching and future repairs.

Related Calculators

  • Paint Calculator
  • Flooring Calculator
  • Concrete Calculator
  • Gravel Calculator
  • Brick Wall Calculator

sections.heading: Why Waste Percentage Matters
sections.content_html:
Why Waste Percentage Matters

Tiles break, edges get cut, and corners require trims. That is why professional tile installers always add 5–15% extra depending on layout complexity.

  • Straight lay: 5–7% waste
  • Diagonal layout: 10–12%
  • Herringbone/complex patterns: 12–15%

sections.heading: Example
sections.content_html:
Example

Room size: 4m × 3m = 12 m²
Tile size coverage per tile: 0.04 m² (e.g., 20cm × 20cm)

12 ÷ 0.04 = 300 tiles baseline

If waste is 10%:
300 × 1.10 = 330 tiles

If each box contains 25 tiles:
330 ÷ 25 = 13.2 → 14 boxes

sections.heading: When to Increase Waste
sections.content_html:
When to Increase Waste

  • Patterned tiles that need directional alignment
  • Small tiles with many cuts
  • Narrow bathrooms with corners and pipes
  • Tiles prone to chipping or cracking during cuts

sections.heading: Tiles Per Box
sections.content_html:
Tiles Per Box

Always check:

  • Coverage per box on the packaging
  • Whether grout lines affect total layout area

Box coverage typically ranges between 1–1.5 m² per box.

sections.heading: FAQs
sections.content_html:
FAQs

How much extra should I buy?
Minimum 5% for straight layouts, up to 15% for patterned layouts.

Does grout change tile count?
Grout lines don’t change area coverage enough to impact total calculation significantly.

Can I calculate for walls and floors?
Yes, the full calculator will support both.

What if tiles go out of stock?
Buying extra prevents mismatched batches or tone differences later.

source_payload.title: Gravel Calculator
source_payload.contentHtml:
Home

Estimate gravel needed for driveways, garden beds, and pathways. Calculate volume and tons required.

Gravel Volume Calculator | Driveway & Garden Fill

Use this gravel calculator to estimate how much gravel you need for any landscaping, driveway, or ground-fill project. Once activated, you will be able to enter total length, width, and depth to receive an instant volume calculation in cubic meters and tons.

Gravel Volume & Weight Calculator

Units:
meters
meters
centimeters
Commonly 5–15% extra for compaction and uneven ground.
Typical gravel density is 1.5–1.7 tonnes per cubic meter.

Enter area size and depth to estimate gravel volume and weight.

This is an approximate calculation. Always check with your supplier which density and delivery unit (tonnes, cubic yards, etc.) they use.

Related Calculators

  • Concrete Calculator
  • Asphalt Calculator
  • Fence Post Calculator
  • Roofing Calculator
  • Brick Wall Calculator

Standard Gravel Depth Guidelines

  • Paths & walkways: 50–75 mm
  • Driveways: 100–150 mm
  • Drainage layers: 150–200 mm

Example

Driveway: 6m × 3m with depth 0.1m

Volume = 6 × 3 × 0.1 = 1.8 m³

If gravel density is ~1.6 tons per m³:
1.8 × 1.6 = 2.88 tons required

Gravel Density

Most gravel types range between 1.5–1.8 tons per m³, depending on:

  • stone type
  • moisture level
  • compaction rate

Tips Before Ordering Gravel

  • Consider compaction: final level settles lower
  • Add 5–10% extra for delivery loss or leveling
  • Ensure weed membrane beneath for clean finish
  • Avoid spreading in heavy rain; water bloats weight

Gravel Types

  • Pea gravel: smooth, decorative
  • Crushed gravel: structural, driveways
  • River rock: larger, landscaping
  • Limestone/granite: compact well, ideal load-bearing

FAQs

How accurate is gravel volume estimation?
It provides standard density-based calculation and is generally accurate for most DIY uses.

Can I convert m³ to tons?
Yes, the live calculator will show both.

Should I add sand beneath gravel?
For driveways or paths, a compacted base layer is recommended.

source_payload.title: Drywall Calculator
source_payload.contentHtml:
Home

Calculate how many drywall sheets you need for walls and ceilings. Includes waste and screw estimates.

Drywall Sheet Calculator | Room & Wall Coverage

This drywall calculator estimates how many sheets are needed for any room, wall, or ceiling installation. When the full tool is activated, you will be able to enter wall dimensions and automatically get sheet count, waste, and screw estimates.

Drywall Sheet Calculator

Units:
meters
meters
meters (floor to ceiling)
Include ceiling if you are boarding the full room.
Choose common board size or set custom.
Use 10–15% for offcuts, openings and mistakes.
meters or feet, based on units
meters or feet, based on units

Enter room size and sheet size to estimate drywall sheets.

This calculator assumes standard rectangular rooms. Irregular shapes, stairwells and complex layouts should be measured separately or checked by a contractor.

Related Calculators

  • Paint Calculator
  • Flooring Calculator
  • Tile Calculator
  • Concrete Calculator
  • Brick Wall Calculator

Standard Sheet Sizes

Common drywall sizes:

  • 1200 × 2400 mm
  • 1200 × 3000 mm
  • 1200 × 3600 mm

Area coverage per sheet:

  • 2.88 m² (standard 1200 × 2400)
  • 3.60 m² (1200 × 3000)
  • 4.32 m² (1200 × 3600)

Example

Room perimeter area: 45 m²
Standard sheet coverage: 2.88 m²

45 ÷ 2.88 ≈ 15.6 → 16 sheets

Add 10% waste:
16 × 1.10 ≈ 18 sheets

When to Add Waste

  • Multiple cuts needed for windows and doors
  • Ceiling mounts where board trimming is required
  • Non-square rooms

Screw Estimate (Approx.)

A common drywall estimate:

  • 50–60 screws per 1200 × 2400mm sheet
  • Ceilings require more due to gravity pull

Moisture-Resistant Use

Use green or blue boards for:

  • Bathrooms
  • Kitchens
  • Laundry rooms
  • Basements

Standard boards are fine for living and bedroom spaces.

FAQs

How much waste should I include?
10% for simple layouts, 12–15% for rooms with multiple openings.

Can I mix board sizes?
Yes, mixing can reduce waste and seams.

Do ceilings need different board types?
Ceilings can use thicker board to prevent sagging.

source_payload.title: Roofing Calculator
source_payload.contentHtml:
Home

Estimate roofing shingles bundles needed based on roof slope, area, and waste percentage.

Roofing Shingles Calculator | Bundles & Waste Estimate

Use this roofing shingles calculator to estimate how many bundles of shingles are required for full roof coverage. Once activated, you will be able to enter roof area, slope, and waste percentage to get a complete estimate.

Roofing Shingle Calculator

Units:
meters along the ridge
meters across the building
Degrees (e.g. 25–45° typical). Leave blank to ignore slope.
Commonly 10–15% extra for cuts, hips and valleys.
Metric: m² per bundle (typical 2.5–3 m²). Imperial: bundles per roofing square (~3).

Enter building size and slope to estimate roofing shingles.

This tool assumes a simple gable roof with two equal slopes. Complex roofs (hips, dormers, multiple sections) should be measured section by section or checked by a roofing professional.

Related Calculators

  • Asphalt Calculator
  • Gravel Calculator
  • Concrete Calculator
  • Paint Calculator
  • Tile Calculator

Shingle Coverage Basics

One bundle of asphalt shingles covers approximately:
3.0 to 3.5 m² depending on brand and style.

Roof area increases with slope:

  • Low slope (10–20°): +5% material
  • Mid slope (20–30°): +10%
  • Steep slope (30°+): +12–15%

Example

Roof area (flat): 100 m²
If slope is mid (25°): add 10%
100 × 1.10 = 110 m² adjusted area

If each bundle covers 3.3 m²:
110 ÷ 3.3 ≈ 33 bundles

Waste Percentage

Standard waste:

  • Straight layout: 8–10%
  • Valleys/edges: 12–15%
  • Complex roof geometry: 15–18%

When to Add Extra

  • Valleys and multiple pitch lines
  • Skylights and chimneys
  • Patterned shingles

Ventilation Note

Roof lifespan depends heavily on attic ventilation and underlayment integrity.

FAQs

How accurate is shingle estimation?
For standard roof shapes and slopes, estimates are typically reliable within ±10%.

Do different shingles vary in coverage?
Yes, always check manufacturer coverage per bundle.

What about hip and ridge caps?
These require additional bundles, typically listed separately on packaging.

source_payload.contentHtml:
Home

Calculate flooring boxes needed based on room area and waste percentage. Simple estimate for laminate, parquet, and vinyl.

Flooring Calculator | Boxes & Waste Estimate

This flooring calculator estimates how many boxes of laminate, vinyl, or parquet flooring you need for any room. When activated, you will be able to enter total room area and waste percentage to receive an accurate box count.

Flooring / Parquet Calculator

Units:
meters
meters
Extra m² or ft² for closets, niches, etc.
Most flooring uses 5–10% extra for cuts and pattern.
Metric: m² per box (often 1.5–2.5 m²). Imperial: ft² per box.

Enter room size to estimate flooring area and boxes.

Check the exact coverage printed on your flooring or parquet packaging and always round up to full boxes.

Related Calculators

  • Tile Calculator
  • Paint Calculator
  • Drywall Calculator
  • Concrete Calculator
  • Brick Wall Calculator

Standard Coverage

Flooring boxes typically cover:

  • 1.8 to 2.3 m² per box (varies by brand and plank size)

Always check package coverage printed on the box before final purchase.

Waste Percentage

Standard waste:

  • Straight plank layout: 5–7%
  • Diagonal layout: 10–12%
  • Herringbone/complex patterns: 12–15%

Example

Room size: 5m × 4m = 20 m²
Waste: 10%
20 × 1.10 = 22 m² total coverage

If each box covers 2.0 m²:
22 ÷ 2.0 = 11 boxes needed

Thickness & Wear Layer

Flooring durability depends on:

  • Thickness (e.g. 8mm, 10mm, 12mm)
  • Wear layer rating (e.g. AC3, AC4, AC5)

Higher AC rating = better resistance to scratches and foot traffic.

Subfloor Preparation

  • Ensure level surface
  • Use moisture barrier if installing over concrete slab
  • Allow acclimatization of planks (24–72 hours in room)

FAQs

How much waste should I add?
Minimum 5% for simple plank installs, 12–15% for patterned layouts.

Does plank thickness change quantity?
No, thickness affects durability, not area coverage.

Can I mix box types?
Avoid mixing batches due to tone variation.

No, thickness affects durability, not area coverage.

Avoid mixing batches due to tone variation.

sections.heading: Flooring Calculator | Boxes & Waste Estimate
sections.content_html:
Flooring Calculator | Boxes & Waste Estimate

This flooring calculator estimates how many boxes of laminate, vinyl, or parquet flooring you need for any room. When activated, you will be able to enter total room area and waste percentage to receive an accurate box count.

sections.heading: Flooring / Parquet Calculator
sections.content_html:
Flooring / Parquet Calculator

Units:
meters
meters
Extra m² or ft² for closets, niches, etc.
Most flooring uses 5–10% extra for cuts and pattern.
Metric: m² per box (often 1.5–2.5 m²). Imperial: ft² per box.

Enter room size to estimate flooring area and boxes.

Check the exact coverage printed on your flooring or parquet packaging and always round up to full boxes.

Related Calculators

  • Tile Calculator
  • Paint Calculator
  • Drywall Calculator
  • Concrete Calculator
  • Brick Wall Calculator

sections.heading: Standard Coverage
sections.content_html:
Standard Coverage

Flooring boxes typically cover:

  • 1.8 to 2.3 m² per box (varies by brand and plank size)

Always check package coverage printed on the box before final purchase.

sections.heading: Waste Percentage
sections.content_html:
Waste Percentage

Standard waste:

  • Straight plank layout: 5–7%
  • Diagonal layout: 10–12%
  • Herringbone/complex patterns: 12–15%

sections.heading: Example
sections.content_html:
Example

Room size: 5m × 4m = 20 m²
Waste: 10%
20 × 1.10 = 22 m² total coverage

If each box covers 2.0 m²:
22 ÷ 2.0 = 11 boxes needed

sections.heading: Thickness & Wear Layer
sections.content_html:
Thickness & Wear Layer

Flooring durability depends on:

  • Thickness (e.g. 8mm, 10mm, 12mm)
  • Wear layer rating (e.g. AC3, AC4, AC5)

Higher AC rating = better resistance to scratches and foot traffic.

sections.heading: Subfloor Preparation
sections.content_html:
Subfloor Preparation

  • Ensure level surface
  • Use moisture barrier if installing over concrete slab
  • Allow acclimatization of planks (24–72 hours in room)

source_payload.title: Asphalt Calculator
source_payload.contentHtml:
Home

Estimate asphalt required for driveways and surfaces. Calculates tons based on area and depth.

Asphalt Volume Calculator | Driveway Tonnage Estimate

This asphalt calculator estimates how much asphalt you need for driveway paving or surface restoration. The full calculator will allow you to enter area and depth, returning both cubic meters and tons.

Asphalt Volume & Tonnage Calculator

Units:
meters
meters
centimeters (typical 5–10 cm)
Commonly 5–10% extra for loss and compaction.
Typical hot mix density is around 2.3–2.5 tonnes per cubic meter.

Enter area size and thickness to estimate asphalt volume and weight.

This is an approximate estimate. Always confirm thickness, density and truck load size with your asphalt supplier or contractor.

Related Calculators

  • Gravel Calculator
  • Roofing Calculator
  • Concrete Calculator
  • Fence Post Calculator
  • Brick Wall Calculator

Standard Asphalt Thickness

Apply depth based on vehicle usage:

  • Light car driveway: 50–75 mm
  • Multi-vehicle/heavy use: 75–100 mm

Density Reference

Standard hot-mix asphalt density:
2.3–2.4 tons per cubic meter

Example

Driveway: 9m × 3m, depth 0.075m (75mm)

Volume = 9 × 3 × 0.075 = 2.025 m³
Weight:
2.025 × 2.35 (average density) ≈ 4.75 tons

Base Layer

A compacted base layer is recommended:

  • Crushed stone or gravel base
  • 100–150 mm thickness

This prevents cracking and early sink points.

Weather Considerations

  • Avoid installation below 10°C
  • Rain affects compaction and adhesion
  • Hot mix cools fast: laying efficiency matters

FAQs

Are asphalt tons estimates accurate?
Yes, for standard mixes ±5% variation.

Can I convert to square meters?
Surface area is measured in square meters, volume in cubic meters, tons are based on density.

Do I need a base layer?
Yes, most driveway failures come from weak base preparation.

source_payload.title: Fence Post Calculator
source_payload.contentHtml:
Home

Estimate concrete needed per fence post, including depth and hole diameter. Simple DIY post footing calculation.

Fence Post Concrete Calculator | Bags per Post

This fence post calculator estimates how many 25 kg concrete bags are required per post. Once activated, you will enter post spacing, hole diameter, and depth to determine concrete volume and bag requirements.

Fence Post Concrete Calculator

Units:
meters
meters between posts
centimeters
centimeters
For overdigging, bell-shaped holes, loss, etc.
Metric: m³ per bag (~0.02 m³ for 25 kg). Imperial: ft³ per bag (~0.6 ft³).

Enter fence length, spacing and hole size to estimate concrete.

This is an approximate estimate based on cylindrical post holes. Always check local frost depth and post size with your builder or supplier.

Related Calculators

  • Concrete Calculator
  • Gravel Calculator
  • Asphalt Calculator
  • Roofing Calculator
  • Brick Wall Calculator

Standard Post Hole Dimensions

General guideline:

  • Hole diameter: 2–3× post width
  • Depth: 1/3 of total post height (minimum)

Example:
Post height: 2.1m
Depth: 2.1 ÷ 3 ≈ 0.70m

Volume Example

Hole diameter: 300mm (0.3m)
Hole depth: 0.70m

Volume = π × (0.15²) × 0.70
≈ 0.049 m³ per post

Bag conversion:
1 m³ ≈ 1000L and 25kg bag covers ≈ 10L

0.049 m³ = 49L
49 ÷ 10 ≈ 5 bags per post

Concrete Mix Tip

Fast-setting post concrete is recommended for:

  • Fences
  • Mailboxes
  • Garden structures

Standard mix is fine but requires bracing and longer set time.

Frequently Asked Questions

How many bags per post on average?
Typically 3–6 bags depending on post size and depth.

Should I use gravel at the bottom?
A 100mm gravel base improves drainage.

Do posts need bracing?
Yes, brace during set time to avoid lean or shift.

source_payload.title: Brick Calculator
source_payload.contentHtml:
Home

Calculate brick count and mortar volume needed for walls. Includes standard brick sizes and waste percentage.

Brick Wall Calculator | Bricks & Mortar Estimate

Use this brick wall calculator to estimate bricks and mortar needed for full wall construction or repairs. When live, you will enter wall length, height, and brick dimensions to get results.

Brick Wall Calculator

Units:
meters
meters
Typical single-skin wall: ~60 bricks per m² (including joints).
Use 5–10% extra for cuts and breakage.
Enter to estimate pallets or packs needed.

Enter wall size to estimate bricks needed.

This calculator assumes a single-skin brick wall with standard mortar joints. For cavity walls, double-skin walls or complex openings, calculate each section separately or consult a mason.

Standard Brick Sizes

Most bricks:

  • 215 × 102.5 × 65 mm

Coverage:

  • ~60 bricks per m² including mortar joints

Example

Wall: 4m × 2.5m = 10 m²
Bricks:
10 × 60 ≈ 600 bricks

Waste:
600 × 1.05 ≈ 630 bricks (5% waste)

Mortar Estimate

Standard mortar:

  • ~0.03 m³ per m² of brickwork

Example:
10 m² × 0.03 = 0.30 m³ mortar

When to Increase Waste

  • Decorative patterns
  • Multiple window/door openings
  • Uneven substrate

FAQs

How many bricks per m²?
Approximately 60 bricks including joints.

What is mortar coverage per m²?
About 0.03 m³ per m² of wall.

Does brick orientation change count?
Patterns like basketweave or herringbone increase waste.

source_payload.contentHtml:
Home

Legal & Privacy Notice

Last Updated: December 2025

This website (“Material Calc Pro”) provides free construction and DIY calculators for informational purposes.

1. Data Collection & Cookies

We do not ask users to create accounts or submit personal information.

However, like most websites, basic technical data may be collected automatically, such as:

  • IP address
  • device and browser type
  • pages visited and duration
  • general location (city/country level)

We may use cookies and similar technologies to maintain site functionality, improve performance, and measure traffic.

If you are located in the EU/EEA or another region requiring consent, a cookie notice may appear when you first access the site.

2. Advertising & Google AdSense

This website may display ads provided by Google AdSense and its third-party advertising partners.

Ads may use cookies to:

  • deliver personalized or non-personalized ads
  • measure ad performance
  • prevent fraud and abuse

You can manage ad personalization through Google settings.

We do not control Google’s cookie usage and do not sell user data.

3. Analytics & Server Logs

We may use basic analytics tools (including Google Analytics or similar) to understand site usage.

Hosting services may generate server logs automatically.

This information is used only to maintain and improve website performance.

We do not identify users personally and do not sell or share this data.

4. Results Disclaimer (Very Important)

All calculators on this website provide estimates only.

Material quantities and construction needs vary due to:

  • installation method
  • waste factors
  • local building codes
  • material density differences
  • soil / slope / weather conditions
  • professional judgment

Always verify with a licensed contractor, builder, or engineer before purchasing materials or beginning work.

We do not provide engineering or architectural advice.

5. No Liability

To the maximum extent permitted by law, we are not responsible for:

  • project errors or material miscalculations
  • damages, loss, or cost arising from use of calculator estimates
  • technical issues, outages, or incorrect data

Use this website at your own discretion and risk.

6. External Links

We may include links to third-party sites.

We do not control or endorse their content, policies, or security practices.

7. Children’s Information

This site is not directed toward children under 13.

We do not knowingly collect data from minors.

8. Contact

For data inquiries, correction, removal, calculator issues, or any other questions:

Email: info@catalitium.com

9. Updates

This page may be updated occasionally to reflect changes in advertising, law, or site functionality.

Continued use of the site after updates indicates acceptance.
